questions.question: Centripetal acceleration is always directed:
options: In the direction of the velocity
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration always points toward the center of the circular path, perpendicular to the velocity.

questions.question: A car moves at $12\text{ m/s}$ around a circular track of radius $48\text{ m}$. What is its centripetal acceleration?
An object moves in a circular path. If its speed is doubled while the radius remains unchanged, what happens to the centripetal acceleration?
questions.explanation: $a_c \propto v^2$. Doubling $v$ means $a_c$ increases by a factor of $2^2 = 4$.

questions.question: A cyclist experiences a centripetal acceleration of $4.5\text{ m/s}^2$ while moving at $9.0\text{ m/s}$ around a bend. What is the radius of the bend?
A race car experiences a centripetal acceleration of $20\text{ m/s}^2$ on a curve of radius $80\text{ m}$. What is its speed?
A ball swings in a horizontal circle at the end of a string at constant speed. Which statement is true?
options: The ball has no acceleration because speed is constant
The acceleration is directed tangent to the circle
The acceleration is directed toward the center of the circle
The acceleration is directed outward from the circle
questions.explanation: Although speed is constant, the velocity changes direction, so there is acceleration. It points toward the center — centripetal acceleration.

questions.question: If the radius of a circular path is halved while speed remains constant, what happens to the centripetal acceleration?
Object X moves at speed $v$ in a circle of radius $r$. Object Y moves at speed $2v$ in a circle of radius $2r$. How does the centripetal acceleration of Y compare to X?
options: Y has half the acceleration of X
Y has the same acceleration as X
Y has twice the acceleration of X
Y has four times the acceleration of X

questions.question: An object moves with constant period $T$ in a circle of radius $r$. If the radius is doubled but the period remains the same, what happens to the centripetal acceleration?
questions.explanation: $v = 2\pi r/T$, so $v \propto r$. $a_c = v^2/r \propto r^2/r = r$. Doubling $r$ doubles $a_c$.

questions.question: A car takes a curve at $20\text{ m/s}$ and experiences a centripetal acceleration of $8.0\text{ m/s}^2$. The same car takes another curve of half the radius at the same speed. What is the new centripetal acceleration?
The Moon orbits Earth in a nearly circular path. What is the direction of the Moon's centripetal acceleration?
options: Tangent to its orbit, in the direction of motion
The Moon has no acceleration because its speed is constant
questions.explanation: The centripetal acceleration of any orbiting object points toward the center of the circular path — in this case, toward Earth's center.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of centripetal acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration always has units of $\text{m/s}^2$, and centripetal acceleration is no exception.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered centripetal acceleration and its relationships!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the proportional relationships $a_c \propto v^2$ and $a_c \propto 1/r$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on using $a_c = v^2/r$ and solving for different variables.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: centripetal acceleration direction, $a_c = v^2/r$, and SI units.
